factors.name: China Industrial Profits
factors.summary: China industrial profits jumped 24.7% YoY in April, fastest in >2 years (YTD +18.2% vs prior +15.5%), buoyed by exports and upstream gains. Supports Dow heavyweights CAT, BA with China demand exposure.

factors.name: Asia Tech/Semiconductor Rally
factors.summary: SK Hynix crossed $1T valuation (+11%), Japan Nikkei hit record highs on chip strength, Samsung union approved wage deal (74% support). Positive spillover for US chip-adjacent Dow names and sentiment backdrop.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Chatter
factors.summary: Unconfirmed reports of US-Iran deal signing, but fresh retaliation threats and Israeli strikes on Lebanon (3,213 killed since March) create persistent uncertainty. Oil dipped to $91.90 WTI on deal hopes, but Hormuz risk remains; energy input-cost signal unclear.

factors.name: Australia CPI Miss
factors.summary: Australia April headline CPI 4.2% YoY vs 4.4% expected, but trimmed-mean core inflation edged to 3.4% (highest since late 2024). Mixed signal: headline relief offset by sticky core, limiting RBA hawkish repricing tailwinds for USD.

factors.name: RBNZ Hawkish Hold
factors.summary: RBNZ held OCR at 2.25% on 3-3 MPC split (governor's casting vote), but signaled rates will rise sooner and more than expected as inflation heads to 4.3%. Supports higher forward rate expectations globally, but immediate impact is NZD strength, not clear US equity driver.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh Fed communication in this cycle; BOJ Governor Ueda warned temporary oil shocks can persist if wages/expectations shift, but no hawkish shift signaled by US Fed. Rate expectations still data-dependent; no directional catalyst evident.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield
factors.summary: No yield data provided in news; last known context suggests rates stable pending inflation data. Oil uncertainty and geopolitical risk keep real yields anchored; no clear rally or collapse in equities from rate repricing.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility
factors.summary: WTI slipped below $92 on peace deal hopes, but Iran retaliation threats and Strait of Hormuz closure risk (25 vessels transited last 24h) create two-way chop. Energy cost inflation risk remains latent for industrial Dow names (CAT, HON, GE).

factors.name: US Political/Regulatory Noise
factors.summary: Trump-backed Ken Paxton toppled Sen. Cornyn in Texas GOP primary; Biden-DOJ audio release dispute ongoing; SEC mulling IPO rule changes (SpaceX/Tesla merger chatter). No immediate Dow-specific earnings or trade policy catalyst visible.

factors.name: Global Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Middle East escalation (Israel-Hezbollah strikes, Iran retaliation threats), Ukraine tensions (Russia plans Kyiv strikes), Bangladesh measles outbreak add to macro uncertainty. VIX backdrop not explicitly given, but tension suggests elevated risk premium.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings releases or forward guidance mentioned in this cycle. Samsung wage deal positive for supply chain, but impact on UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA guidance unknown.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (DXY)
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8291 vs 6.7883 estimate (weaker yuan reference). Mixed FX signals (NZD up post-RBNZ, GBP holding above 1.3450, EUR flat at 1.1640). No clear DXY directional move; multinational translation effects neutral.

screening.narrative: US30 is choppy near 50,462 (-0.21% in 24h, +0.15% in 4h) as Asia-led semiconductor and China growth optimism (industrial profits +24.7% YoY, Nikkei/SK Hynix records) mix with persistent Middle East escalation (Iran retaliation threats, Israel-Hezbollah strikes) and latent oil/energy cost risks. Peace deal whispers cap upside, but unconfirmed status and fresh hostilities keep volatility high. Fed policy backdrop neutral pending next CPI/FOMC; no Dow heavyweight earnings catalyst visible this cycle.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI or PCE inflation release (likely early June, monitor for timing)
ISM Manufacturing PMI and Services PMI (critical for Dow industrial composition)
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production (real economy reads)
Fed FOMC statement and rate guidance (if scheduled within 72h)
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA) — monitor calendars
US-Iran deal finalization or breakdown (binary geopolitical catalyst for oil/risk assets)
China PMI and policy updates (May official manufacturing PMI due early June)
RBNZ follow-up messaging or other central bank guidance (rate expectations repricing)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran peace deal collapses or Hormuz is closed, oil could spike above $100, triggering stagflation fears and Dow weakness (input costs on industrials, dividend payer yields repricing). Conversely, if peace deal stabilizes and China growth reaccelerates into Q2 earnings, Dow could break above 50,700 on cyclical strength. Major Fed surprise (rate cut signals vs current expectations) or surprise hot CPI could flip to SELL bias quickly.
screening.entry_guidance: Current action is shallow and uncertain; avoid chasing above 50,700 resistance. If deal fears ease and Asian momentum continues, watch for a retest of 50,500–50,550 support as a cleaner long entry. For shorts, require a break below 50,250 on geopolitical spike or unexpected US inflation hot surprise.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari (May 27, 07:23) stated inflation risk is now higher than labor market deterioration risk, with 'most US data since April showing inflationary risks are higher, not lower.' This hawkish signal suggests no near-term rate cuts, pressuring dividend-heavy Dow names and rate-sensitive cyclicals.

factors.name: China Industrial Growth
factors.summary: China's industrial profits jumped 24.7% y/y in April (May 27, 01:36), fastest in over 2 years, signaling robust demand for Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE). Strong upstream energy and export momentum supports global growth narrative.

factors.name: US Inflation Pressure
factors.summary: Kashkari's warning of persistent inflationary shockwaves from Middle East conflict and higher-than-expected inflation risks contrasts with market positioning. This pressures equity valuations and raises real-yield concerns for dividend payers.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US)
factors.summary: Renewed US strikes on Iran (May 27, 06:56) despite ceasefire framework talks have escalated uncertainty around Strait of Hormuz disruptions and energy supply chains. Elevated volatility and supply-chain risk dampen risk-on sentiment, particularly for globally-exposed Dow names.

factors.name: Oil Price Weakness
factors.summary: WTI slumped below $91.00 (May 27, 04:56) on US-Iran peace progress, reducing input cost pressure. This is mildly supportive for Dow industrials (lower transportation costs), but limited upside given geopolitical tail risks remain.

factors.name: European Demand Softness
factors.summary: French consumer confidence fell to lowest since March 2023 (May 27, 07:53), down 9 points in 3 months since US-Iran conflict start. This signals deteriorating demand for Dow's ~50% international revenue base, particularly in discretionary/travel-linked sectors.

factors.name: Tech/AI Momentum (Dow-adjacent)
factors.summary: SK Hynix hit $1 trillion valuation (May 27, 07:21), Micron crossed same threshold; AI-driven chip demand remains robust. Though not direct Dow constituents, positive spillover supports cyclical sentiment and investor risk appetite.

factors.name: Dollar Index Weakness
factors.summary: USD Index softened near 99.00 (May 27, 04:04) on Iran conflict uncertainty, mildly supportive for US multinationals' earnings translation. However, weakness is modest and largely offset by real-yield headwinds from Kashkari's hawkish inflation warnings.

factors.name: 10-Year Yield Dynamics
factors.summary: Bonds rebounded on Iran optimism (May 27, 05:46), suggesting yield compression; however, Kashkari's inflation warnings and persistent 'higher inflation risk' narrative likely keep real yields elevated. Rate-sensitive Dow dividend leaders (utilities, REITs exposure) face headwinds.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: Dow +0.15% in 4h, -0.21% in 24h shows choppy consolidation with no clear directional commitment. Mixed intraday action reflects conflicting narratives (China strength vs. Fed hawkishness, Iran de-escalation vs. supply risk).

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between conflicting macro signals: China's robust 24.7% industrial profit growth and AI-chip strength support cyclical optimism, while Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari's hawkish inflation warnings (May 27) and deteriorating French consumer confidence suggest persistent stagflationary pressures. Renewed US-Iran military escalation despite ceasefire framework talks adds geopolitical uncertainty and supply-chain risk, pressuring multinational earnings and consumer discretionary sentiment. The index is consolidating within a narrow 0.36% range (4h: +0.15%, 24h: -0.21%), reflecting genuine two-way tension with no clear breakout direction.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran framework agreement finalization or breakdown (expected within 24-48h per news sources)
Updated Fed rate expectations following Kashkari's hawkish inflation signals
Upcoming ISM Manufacturing and Services PMI (early June)
US durable goods orders and factory output (late May/early June)
Dow component earnings revisions (cyclicals like CAT, BA, GE in focus given China strength but Fed hawkishness)
European PMI and sentiment data (ongoing weakness in consumer confidence)
Oil supply disruption risk from Hormuz corridor uncertainty
screening.risk_warning: A rapid US-Iran 'deal' announcement with clear Hormuz de-escalation would likely trigger a short-squeeze rally (risk-on reversion), invalidating the bearish geopolitical bias. Conversely, if Fed speakers pivot dovish on real inflation fears or if Chinese growth disappoints in June data, the bullish cyclical support crumbles. The Dow's price-weighted sensitivity to Boeing, Caterpillar, and Goldman Sachs means any guidance cuts from aerospace/industrials or financial margin warnings would spike downside quickly.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is mixed-to-choppy (PHASE_2); avoid fresh longs/shorts. On a break below 50,200 support with close below on daily, consider shorts targeting 49,800 if Fed hawkishness dominates; on a break above 50,700 with Iran de-escalation confirmation, consider longs to 51,200. Wait for either a geopolitical catalyst resolution or ISM data clarity before committing size.

factors.name: AI Optimism & Tech Momentum
factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record highs on AI enthusiasm; SK Hynix and Micron both crossed $1T valuations. Dow has meaningful tech/industrial exposure (MSFT, UNH, AAPL via basket), benefiting from AI capex cycle.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Narrative
factors.summary: Market pricing cautious optimism on de-escalation talks; lower oil prices ($70s range per headlines) reduce input costs for industrials and transportation. Barclays notes peace deal not yet fully priced, suggesting room for relief rally.

factors.name: Weekly ADP Employment (35.75K vs 42.25K prior)
factors.summary: Significant miss in weekly ADP Pulse suggests labor market softening. This supports Fed pause/cut narrative but also signals potential demand weakness ahead—headwind for cyclical Dow names (CAT, BA, DE).

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Repricing
factors.summary: Markets now pricing ~70% probability of at least one rate hike by year-end (Goldman Sachs, BlackRock shifted hawkish). Fed's Kashkari warned inflation still 'far too soon' to declare victory. Higher-for-longer rates pressure dividend/yield-dependent Dow names.

factors.name: Mortgage Rates & Housing Data
factors.summary: US mortgage rates hit highest level since August; refinance demand dropped 18%. Weak housing data pressures home-builders and consumer discretionary (HD, relevant Dow exposure), signals demand fragility.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & JPY Weakness
factors.summary: USD/JPY near 160 (intervention risk); DXY flatlines at 99.0. Strong dollar is a headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue, but currency moves have been modest; no clear directional catalyst yet.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline & Commodity Weakness
factors.summary: Brent oil retreated from Monday spike on de-escalation hopes. Lower oil reduces input costs (positive for margins) but signals weaker global demand and risk appetite caution. CVX exposure in Dow is muted vs SPY.

factors.name: Inflation/Price Pressure Warnings
factors.summary: Food companies eyeing margin protection and Walmart raising prices (per Bloomberg); Fed officials cite inflation persistence. This contradicts soft-landing thesis and supports higher-for-longer rates, pressuring equities.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Context
factors.summary: No explicit VIX level provided, but geopolitical uncertainty (US-Iran, Gaza, Russia) and mixed macro data suggest VIX likely 16-20 range—not panic, not complacency. Choppy conditions ahead.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Limited forward guidance in news; notable: Micron, SK Hynix rallying hard on AI demand, but Zscaler tumbled on FY27 growth concerns. Mixed signals within Dow heavyweights; no clear confirmation.

screening.narrative: The Dow is riding a wave of AI optimism and peace-deal hopes after record closes in S&P 500/Nasdaq, with US futures up 0.3% and EUR/USD near weekly highs. However, underlying macro cracks are widening: ADP employment data missed significantly (35.75K vs 42.25K), mortgage rates climbed to 9-month highs, and Fed officials are increasingly hawkish on inflation persistence, pricing in ~70% odds of at least one rate hike by year-end. The move higher has already begun (S&P 500 at all-time highs), placing the Dow in Phase 2 territory—the narrative is constructive on near-term sentiment, but execution is fragile without confirmation from Dow heavyweights' earnings and forward guidance.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (due this week) — critical for Dow's industrial composition
Durable goods orders & factory orders — direct read on CAT, BA, GE demand
Fed speakers (Kashkari already warned on inflation) — potential for renewed hawkish rhetoric
Key Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, JPM, BA expected late week/early June)
China PMI/economic data — critical for CAT, Boeing, materials-exposed names
FOMC meeting expectations (next ~3 weeks) — rate hike odds will dominate if data stays hot
US-Iran deal outcome (binary event, potentially high-impact on oil, risk appetite)
screening.risk_warning: If ADP/employment data continues to miss and Fed pivots to hawkish rate hikes (vs cut expectations), Dow's dividend payers and rate-sensitive financials will face sharp selloff. Conversely, if US-Iran deal falls apart or energy crisis re-escalates, commodity/energy shock could derail margins and trigger risk-off. Watch Dow-heavy earnings this week—any guidance cuts on margins or capex will invalidate the AI/peace narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (S&P records, Nasdaq up 0.4%); Dow at +0.15% 4h suggests lagging. Wait for retest of 50200–50300 support or confirmation from ISM/durable goods before adding longs; if break above 50700, reassess on earnings strength rather than sentiment.

factors.name: Sector Rotation / Tech Weakness
factors.summary: Tech sector tumbling (NVDA -2.24%, INTL -4.30%) while healthcare and consumer cyclicals lead; Dow's defensive/value tilt favors outperformance vs broad indices. Defensive positioning supports modest Dow upside.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal Noise
factors.summary: Markets opened higher on Iran MoU draft reports, but White House indirectly denied deal progress; gains have faded. Conflicting headlines create chop and reduce conviction. No concrete agreement reached.

factors.name: Oil Price Softness
factors.summary: Crude trimming losses on Iran deal speculation but remains under pressure. Lower energy costs reduce CVX (Dow component) earnings; however, input cost relief could help industrials. Net effect mixed.

factors.name: Richmond Fed May Composite Index
factors.summary: Richmond Fed composite +13 vs +3 prior; manufacturing shipments +16 vs -2 prior; employment expectations rose to 23 from 7. Solid regional manufacturing strength supports industrial demand.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh Fed data release in last 24h. Markets pricing in rate hike bets but ECB likely June hike taking focus. No shift in Fed narrative from recent reads.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & M&A
factors.summary: JPMorgan CEO signals $20B M&A appetite, Eli Lilly buying vaccine makers for ~$4B, Boeing met 737 Max production ramp requirements. Deal flow and capex confidence support cyclical momentum.

factors.name: Inflation Signals & Input Costs
factors.summary: Sugar/fertilizer prices rising (El Niño threat, Peru halts anchovy fishing); grocery prices rose most in nearly 4 years in April; Rhine water levels restricting fuel barge capacity. Stagflationary pressure on margins.

factors.name: Currency & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD mixed; DXY softening on Iran deal optimism but no major directional commitment. Euro +0.21% on risk-on sentiment. Dow's 50% international revenue exposure faces headwinds if USD weakens further.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & Supply Chains
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz traffic faded to crawl; Israel escalating Lebanon strikes; Russia casualty figures high (500k+); ongoing Middle East conflict disrupting trade. Risk-off backdrop headwind for cyclicals.

factors.name: Consumer / Retail Sentiment
factors.summary: Abercrombie beat earnings but EMEA sales down 10% due to Middle East conflict. Dick's Sporting Goods cut FY EPS guidance. Mixed signals; no clear trend in consumer health.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Dow +0.20% at open, now flat to slightly negative over 24h (-0.21%). Minimal conviction move; lacks follow-through. Suggests consolidation rather than fresh trend.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between conflicting narratives: solid regional manufacturing data (Richmond Fed) and M&A optimism from heavyweights like JPMorgan and Eli Lilly support cyclical demand, yet elevated geopolitical risk (Iran, Lebanon escalation), persistent input-cost inflation (sugar, fertilizer, energy), and tech sector weakness create headwinds. US-Iran peace deal chatter has faded after initial hype, removing a near-term risk-off catalyst but leaving uncertainty intact. Sector rotation favoring defensives over tech naturally props the value-heavy Dow, but the lack of sustained price momentum (only -0.21% in 24h, flat on 4h) signals the market is waiting for clearer catalysts before committing directionally.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation data (if released in next 48h — inflation trajectory critical for Dow rate-sensitive names)
US jobs report (NFP typically scheduled for first Friday of month; manufacturing employment and wage growth)
FOMC meeting minutes or next policy statement (Fed rate path for industrials/financials)
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, BA, CAT guidance on capex/demand outlook)
China economic data (NBS manufacturing PMI, industrial production — demand signal for Caterpillar, Boeing, materials)
Iran peace deal confirmation or breakdown (geopolitical re-risk, oil spike could hurt equity multiples)
ECB rate decision (11 June per reports; affects USD, EM, global growth sentiment)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would invalidate if: (1) US-Iran deal formally breaks down, causing oil spike and risk-off capitulation (Dow would underperform broad indices); (2) PCE or CPI surprises hot, forcing Fed higher and crushing valuations; (3) major Dow component (JPMorgan, Boeing, Caterpillar) misses on guidance and triggers rotation back to tech; (4) Geopolitical escalation (Israel-Hezbollah, Russia-Ukraine) spike VIX >25, forcing deleveraging.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is exhausted and undecided (only -0.21% in 24h after initial Iran deal hype). Avoid chasing; wait for either a pullback to 50,500 support (potential dip-buy on RSI oversold) or break above 51,000 resistance with volume confirmation before adding conviction. Richmond Fed strength supports a bias for higher over next 1-2 weeks, but requires near-term consolidation first.

factors.name: Iran Deal Uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump explicitly rejected Iran sanctions relief and stated 'we're not there yet' on a deal (16:06 UTC), erasing earlier optimism from Iran's draft MoU. Market swung from relief-bid to cautious skepticism within hours, leaving equities stuck.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility
factors.summary: Oil fell >5% on Rubio's 'give talks every chance' comment, then stabilized as Trump's hardline stance reintroduced geopolitical risk premium. Near-term Strait of Hormuz closure remains a supply shock tail risk.

factors.name: AI Sector Rotation / Sector Divergence
factors.summary: Tech tumbled with Nvidia -2.24% and Intel -4.30% (per 14:51 UTC report), while healthcare and consumer cyclicals led. Micron's trillion-dollar milestone masks broader AI trade exhaustion; S&P 500 and Nasdaq 'muted' while Dow holds defensives near record.

factors.name: Dow Record High / Defensive Lean
factors.summary: Dow reported at record high near 50,700 (18:05 UTC), outperforming S&P 500 and Nasdaq. Index composition (industrials, financials) benefits from rate-hold scenario and potential M&A wave (Dimon $10-20B, Lilly $20B+ spree, Uber/Delivery Hero).

factors.name: Treasury Yields and Rate Expectations
factors.summary: 5-year auction at 4.182% vs 4.181% WI; yields have fallen since May 19 highs amid Iran optimism, but Trump's hardline may re-steepen. No Fed decision imminent; next FOMC is weeks away. Rate hike bets building per FX commentary.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY recovered intraday losses as Trump rejected Iran deal (18:19 UTC), supporting the Greenback. Stronger USD typically headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue, but nearterm geopolitical bid for safety offsets.

factors.name: Earnings Momentum & M&A Activity
factors.summary: JPMorgan, Lilly, Uber, Boeing all signaling strong spending, capex, and acquisition appetite. Abercrombie beat (12% jump on 18:05 UTC) despite MENA headwinds; United Airlines eyes double-digit margins 2027. Cyclical confidence intact.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Iran conflict unresolved; Strait of Hormuz closed; Israel striking Hezbollah targets (17:12, 17:14 UTC). Traders skeptical of rapid Hormuz normalization. Supply shock to energy and food inflation remains if conflict re-escalates.

factors.name: Food Insecurity & Consumer Bifurcation
factors.summary: NY Fed reports 'remarkable rise' in food insecurity for low-income households (19:14 UTC); K-shaped economy widening. Consumer confidence resilience noted, but underlying stress signals mixed sentiment for Dow consumer names.

factors.name: Tariff Speculation & Trade Policy
factors.summary: Copper traders scouring globe amid renewed tariff speculation (15:53 UTC); Canada choosing Swedish jets over US model (18:43 UTC). Trade war tail risk simmers but no new Trump tariff announcements in this window.

factors.name: Market Breadth & Internals
factors.summary: Dow holding record while S&P 500 and Nasdaq 'muted' or weak suggests narrow leadership. Tech weakness and sector rotation signal tired broad-market momentum despite headline Dow gain.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h +0.05%, 24h -0.17%
factors.summary: Dow flat to marginally up on intraday basis; 24h loss of -17 bps suggests pullback after earlier record. Move is choppy, consistent with Phase 2 consolidation amid conflicting catalysts.

screening.narrative: The Dow Jones scaled a record high near 50,700 earlier today, buoyed by defensive positioning, M&A commentary (Dimon, Lilly, Boeing), and strong earnings beats (Abercrombie +12%). However, the index has retreated modestly (-17 bps on 24h basis) as the broader market grapples with conflicting Iran deal signals: initial optimism on a draft MoU was erased by Trump's explicit rejection of sanctions relief and assertion that 'we're not there yet.' Meanwhile, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have turned muted as the AI trade wobbles (Nvidia -2.24%, Intel -4.30%), rotating into healthcare and consumer cyclicals. The Dow's outperformance masks narrow breadth; the confluence of geopolitical tail risk (Strait of Hormuz still closed, Israel-Hezbollah escalation), persistent food insecurity concerns, and tech sector rotation creates a mixed tactical backdrop.
catalysts_ahead: US jobless claims (weekly, likely tomorrow or Thu)
PCE / core PCE inflation data (pending release timing)
ISM Services PMI (if not yet released)
Boeing/GE/Caterpillar forward guidance updates
JPMorgan, UnitedHealth, MSFT earnings calls / guidance
Iran deal outcome or Trump announcement
US Treasury auction results (rate expectations)
China macro data (PMI, trade, industrial production)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal is suddenly announced and Strait of Hormuz reopens, oil collapses further, real yields compress, and the flight-to-safety/defensive bid unwinds—Dow could underperform tech on multiple expansion and lower rates. Conversely, if geopolitical escalation widens (Israel-Iran direct conflict) or Trump announces hard tariffs, dollar rallies hard and multinational earnings guidance weakens, pressuring the Dow despite defensive composition. Tech weakness continuing could also spill into financials (JPM, GS) if equity market breadth deteriorates and credit spreads widen.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (intraday rebound off record, now consolidated near 50,700). Wait for retest of 50,500 support or a break above 50,850 resistance with >1% breadth confirmation before adding longs; avoid chasing flat 4h action into high geopolitical/earnings uncertainty.
